codeReview.summary: The obs-langfuse-export feature is adequately addressed through the Langfuse module implementation (commit 48f03a1) and subsequent OTLP wiring (commit 9da0572). The Langfuse SDK integration exists with proper OTLP/HTTP export to Langfuse's ingestion endpoint, generation usage tracking, and cost data export. Tests (9) pass matching the langfuse_export filter. The prior blocking issues (OtelConfig never instantiated, spans never created in LLM backends) are resolved: commit 9da0572 wires OtelConfig::from_env() and init_tracer_provider() at daemon startup; commits ab1718e and 9fef3f8 instrument LLM backends with GenAI spans that are exported via the configured OTLP endpoint.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The obs-langfuse-export feature has status='pending' and workerSessionIds=[] in features.json, indicating no worker has executed this feature yet. The Langfuse code was committed directly to main (48f03a1) rather than being executed by a mission worker. This creates a disconnect between the feature tracking and actual implementation.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: features.json shows obs-langfuse-export with status: 'pending' and workerSessionIds: [], but the implementation exists in commit 48f03a1 which was done outside the mission worker system.

fixAdequacy: adequate - The obs-langfuse-export feature provides Langfuse SDK integration separate from OTLP. The prior blocking issues from opentelemetry-full (OtelConfig not instantiated, no span creation) are addressed by commit 9da0572 which wires daemon startup to call OtelConfig::from_env() and init_tracer_provider(). The LLM backends (anthropic.rs, openai.rs) now create GenAI spans with proper attributes (gen_ai.operation.name, gen_ai.provider.name, gen_ai.request.model, gen_ai.usage.input_tokens, etc.) that are exported via the configured OTLP endpoint. The Langfuse module provides an alternative export path via HTTP OTLP to Langfuse's ingestion API.
